LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS COX, CASTLE & NICHOLSON, VIGILANT INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STAY OR, ALTERNATIVELY, DENY ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. BECAUSE THE PARTIES DID NOT EXPRESSLY AGREE TO THE PROCEDURAL PROVISIONS OF THE FEDERAL ARBITRATION ACT, THE PROCEDURAL PROVISIONS OF THE CALIFORNIA ARBITRATION ACT-INCLUDING CCP SECTION 1281.2(C)-APPLY. PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1281.2(C), THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS ARE STAYED PENDING RESOLUTION OF THE LITIGATION GIVEN THE RISK OF CONFLICTING RULINGS THAT COULD RESULT IF THE PROCEEDINGS WERE PERMITTED TO PROGRESS SIMULTANEOUSLY. THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY EMAIL. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)
